title: ADR 0001 task 4 done — unit-test runner wired (ai-bookkeeper)
content: ai-bookkeeper now has a wired unit-test runner: tsx@^4 devDependency + `npm test`/`test:watch` scripts running `tsx --test "src/**/*.test.ts"` on Node's built-in test runner. Chose tsx over vitest because amount.test.ts already targets node:test (zero new framework surface). amount.test.ts validator suite = 12/12 green. ADR 0001 updated with an addendum; task 4 of its breakdown is complete (8/15 pts done). No GitHub Actions workflow exists yet, so `npm test` is the CI-ready entry point but not yet in any pipeline. Tasks 5 (SMS/OCR adapter) & 6 (Plaid adapter) remain blocked — those ingestion adapters are still not in the repo, so validation can't be wired to a real entry point yet. Extend ADR 0001, don't re-open the signed-number-vs-integer-cents decision (still deferred to persistence ADR).

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 07:45 UTC
ai-bookkeeper has split unit-test runners — npm test covers only 1 of 5 suites
content: Review of ADR 0001 task 4 (test-runner wiring): verdict REQUEST_CHANGES. The architect added `npm test` = `tsx --test src/**/*.test.ts` (node:test) for amount.test.ts and claimed 'zero new test-framework surface'. That's inverted: the repo's EXISTING convention for pure-logic unit tests is @playwright/test files under e2e/ — 4 suites already exist (benchmarks-categorize, benchmarks-compare, customers-normalize, wealth-score-compute .unit.spec.ts), run via `npm run e2e` (playwright testDir ./e2e). So `npm test` (12/12 green, verified exit 0) covers ONLY the ledger validator and gives false coverage confidence; the other 4 unit suites are excluded by the glob. Recommended unifying on one runner before treating npm test as THE unit gate. SEPARATELY: the prior open finding (amount.ts:77 Number() accepts '1e5'->100000 / '0x10'->16) is STILL unresolved on second review — amount.ts unchanged, and the newly-wired suite has no test for it. Escalated as recurring; must land /^\d+(\.\d{1,2})?$/ guard + test before task 5 (SMS/OCR adapter) feeds untrusted strings. Tasks 5-6 still blocked (adapters absent).

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 07:49 UTC
ai-bookkeeper security baseline — 0 critical, recurring input-boundary coercion gap
content: First full security scan of ai-bookkeeper (Next 16/React 19/TS, frontend-only — no API routes, server actions, AI calls or DB wired; Plaid/Twilio/Gemini adapters declared in .env.example but unbuilt). Result: 0 critical, 0 high, 3 medium, 1 low. (1) npm audit = 2 moderate dep CVEs: postcss<8.5.10 XSS (and next transitively) — fix via npm audit fix / dedupe postcss>=8.5.10. (2) amount.ts:77 Number(cleaned) accepts scientific/hex/binary literals ('1e5'->100000, '0x10'->16) at the untrusted-money-input boundary — recurring (raised twice in code review), MUST fix with /^\d+(\.\d{1,2})?$/ guard before task 5 SMS/OCR adapter. (3) no CSP/security headers in next.config. POSITIVE: clean secret hygiene (.env/.gcp/*.pem gitignored, no hardcoded creds), no eval/exec/XSS sinks, no PII logging. The real authn/PII/injection surface arrives with the backend adapters — re-scan then.

ai-bookkeeper versioning convention — 0.x breaking changes bump MINOR
content: ai-bookkeeper follows pre-1.0 (0.x) semver: BREAKING changes increment the MINOR, not the MAJOR (a 1.0.0 cut is reserved for the API-stability commitment, not toolchain work). Released v0.2.0 on 2026-05-27 (bump 0.1.0→0.2.0): a platform-migration release with breaking framework-major upgrades (Next 14→16, React 18→19, Tailwind 3→4, TS 5→6, ESLint 9→10, Gemini SDK 0.24) but NO product feature changes. CHANGELOG follows Keep a Changelog format with Changed/Fixed sections. Note: the release had been left half-done — CHANGELOG entry drafted but package.json not bumped and no tag — so always verify version file AND tag match the changelog. No git tags existed before v0.2.0; v0.2.0 is the first tag, annotated, on HEAD 18fab0f.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 07:55 UTC
v0.2.0 docs sync is idempotent — re-triggers are no-ops
content: The ai-bookkeeper release.version.bumped event for v0.2.0 (commit 18fab0f) can fire more than once. The README + CHANGELOG 0.2.0 edits already exist in the working tree and repo HEAD remains 18fab0f (== last-processed). When HEAD equals the stored last-processed commit, the correct outcome is 'docs already current' — no edits. The 0.2.0 doc edits remain uncommitted (HEAD still 18fab0f, package.json still 0.1.0); the actual version-bump commit appears still pending on the Release Manager side.
